blocks.md: Text is just an array of characters, written in single quotes. All the array tools you know work on it: shape, reverse, catenate, compare.
Comparing characters gives a Boolean mask, so counting a letter is just `+/ text = 'x'`.
`⍕` (format) turns a value into its text representation; `⍎` (execute) does the reverse — it runs a string as APL code.

blocks.prompt: Report how many characters are in 'mississippi'. Expect 11.
Count how many times the letter s appears in 'mississippi'. Expect 4.
blocks.hint: compare against 's' to get a mask, then sum it

blocks.prompt: Use ⍎ to evaluate the string '2 + 3 × 4' as APL code. Expect 14.
blocks.hint: ⍎ runs the text as an expression
